behavior_in_task_description.reason: All assert'd literals (e.g., 'retries_ctl.isRetry()', 'current_zookeeper = zookeeper_getter.getZooKeeper().first', 'Renew the session on retry', 're-fetch the object list', 'watches are set on the live session', '5-second sleep in processWatchQueue', 'zkutil::ZooKeeperPtr current_zookeeper = zookeeper;') are explicitly named in instruction.md's Required Implementation section.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies exact variable names ('current_zookeeper'), exact code patterns ('retries_ctl.isRetry()'), exact assignment expressions ('zookeeper_getter.getZooKeeper().first'), exact comment strings, and exactly what to move where. This is effectively dictating the solution, not just describing what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: exact variable name 'current_zookeeper' (test_current_zookeeper_variable_exists checks 'zkutil::ZooKeeperPtr current_zookeeper = zookeeper;'), exact comment strings ('Renew the session on retry', 're-fetch the object list', 'watches are set on the live session'), and exact expression 'current_zookeeper = zookeeper_getter.getZooKeeper().first'. An alternative correct fix using e.g. 'fresh_zookeeper' or 'renewed_session' would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Nearly all F2P tests (test_session_renewal_logic_exists, test_object_names_moved_into_retry_loop, test_current_zookeeper_variable_exists, test_tryLoadObject_uses_current_zookeeper, test_comment_explains_session_refresh) are purely text-matching with 'assert X in content' on file contents. test_cpp_syntax_valid runs clang but only checks for syntax errors, not behavioral correctness. No test actually executes the fixed code path or verifies runtime behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: All F2P tests check for literal string presence in file content. A stub that simply writes the expected strings/comments into the file without implementing any real fix would pass. For example, test_session_renewal_logic_exists just checks 'if (retries_ctl.isRetry())' appears in file text - inserting it in a dead code block or comment would pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 7 required implementation points from instruction.md (session renewal check, fresh session acquisition, variable naming, move object_names, use current_zookeeper, comment updates, remove old comment) are tested by the test suite.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_session_renewal_logic_exists, test_object_names_moved_into_retry_loop, etc.), grouped into F2P and P2P categories with section comments, and each has a docstring explaining what it verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task involves C++ code modification, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, variable names, or commands across instruction.md and test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names the exact function (refreshObjects), exact class (UserDefinedSQLObjectsZooKeeperStorage), exact variable names to use (current_zookeeper), exact method calls (retries_ctl.isRetry(), zookeeper_getter.getZooKeeper().first), and exact comments to add/remove. This goes far beyond describing what's broken and effectively dictates the implementation.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd-edits/config task.
